def: part. adj. Free, unfettered, unrestrained, uncontrolled; liberated, discharged, set free, ransomed, emancipated; free born; free from care, at ease, light-hearted; — s.m. more: n. m. 1. A freeman; freedman; 2. A Mohamedan devotee who shaves his beard and eyelashes and takes a vow of continence, but considers himself exempt from the usual observances of religion; a free thinker. 3. An impudent or shameless person (āzād kā soṅṭā). āzād' , adj. 1. Acquitted; at large; loose; scot free; unrestrained; emancipated; set free; manumitted; unchecked; released; set at liberty. 2. Let go; let out; discharged; ransomed. 3. Independent; uncontrolled; free born; unenslaved. 4. Disencumbered; freed; disburdened; unburdened. 5. At ease; free from care; light hearted; at home. āzād kā soṅṭā . The faqīr's staff (an emblem of the āzād's calling). āzād kar'nā , v. a. 1. To set at liberty; set free; deliver; untie; disenthrall; manumit; release; emancipate; liberate. 1. Parāë barde āzād karte haiṅ . He emancipates another's slaves. (To cut thongs from another man's hide). 2. Hamāre…

1. adj. Free, unfettered, liberated. 2. Solitary, lonely. 3. s. m. A kind of faḳīr who shaves his beard, eye-lashes and eye-brows, and vows chastity; a hermit. ازادکرنا āzād karnā, To manumit, set at liberty, emancipate, deliver, free.

def: adj. & s.m. Brown, tan-coloured ( = bhūrā); bald-headed through disease; large, great; — brown colour; any creature or object of a brown colour; a large ichneumon; an epithet of Kr̤ishṇa or Vishṇu, and of Śiva; fire; name of a sage.
n. f. 1. A kiss; a buss. (Used in familiar talk by lovers). 2. (Etawah). A woman's breast; chest.
wety: باب (bāb, “the Báb”) + ـی (-ī)

def: s.m. = bāʼe-birang, q.v. s.f. Lady, mistress, madam; woman, wife (local; cf. bāʼīkā); — dancing-girl, harlot; mother bawd, la bonne
subs: s.f. Mother bawd, the old abbess (syn. nāyikā).

def: s.f. A snake's hole ( = bāṅbhī, q.v.). s.f. Hillock or ground thrown up by white ants, ant-hill; snake's hole (since ant-hills deserted by ants are commonly taken possession of by snakes).
more: s. f. A snakeʼs hole. See بانبهني.

def: postpos. m. (dakh.) By reason, on the subject, in respect.
s.f. Noisy altercation, dissension, squabbling, quarrelling.

s.f. Cropped or dressed hair, fore-locks, tresses; a particular method of dressing the front hair (of a girl or woman); fringe of hair worn on the forehead (by women); cropped hair (mane and forelock) of a horse; curly hair of a camel.
more: n. f. 1. Cropped hair (particularly of a horse). 2. A lock of hair; tresses.
s. f. 1. Cropped hair, tresses. 2. The act of cropping (especially, the manes of horses).
adj. Relating to the lion or tiger.
s. f. Hair of a camel.

def: s.m. Head of hair (a man's, — long and uncut).
s.m. Awkward or clumsy young man, clown, lout, bumpkin (syn. gabrū).
more: s. m. A clown, a lout, lumpkin, clumsy young man.

ety: S. वप्रा or वप्र+उक; cf. bābā
def: s.m. Prince, noble, man of family or distinction; a title of respect (as) Sir, Mr., Esqre.; young master; father; a term of endearment applied to children; — a clerk or writer in an office.
more: n. m. S. वप्र. 1. A title of respect, as Sir, Mr., Esq., young master, father, Sire. 2. A man of family or distinction. Gurū hai hamārā, Rājā re bābū . Song. 3. A clerk or writer in an office. 4. A term of endearment applied to children. bābū bhaīyā , E. n. m. A gentleman; a man of rank and dignity. Bābū bhaīyā ke āge lajāë- ke chāhīṅ . E.
s. m. 1. A child. 2. A prince. 3. Master (a title given by Hindūs, equivalent to Mr. or squire).
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀩𑀸𑀩𑁆𑀩- (*bābba-), imitative of baby talk.

def: prop. n. Babel, or Babylon.

s.f. The gum-acacia tree, Mimosa arabica (the wood of which is commonly used in making cart-wheels, agricultural implements, tent-pegs, &c.; and the bark, being a powerful astringent, is used in tanning: syn. kīkar, mug̠īlān)
more: n. m. S. वर्वुर. Acacia arabica , the wood of which is much used in making cart wheels, agricultural implements, etc. and the bark is used in tanning leather, and in making wine ( kīkar ). Nadī kināre ghanī baburī re, ghanī baburī, Ghaṭā chhoṛ de, sipaiyā, bharūṅ gagrī . E. Wom. Song. Jhūmar . Fast by the river bank the acacia trees are thick, Pray, soldier, stand aside, I'll fill my pitcher quick. babūl ke peṛ bonā , v. a lit . To plant the babūl tree. To do something conductive to a bad end. Karnī kare to kyoṅ ḍare, kar-ke kyoṅ pachhtāë? Boë peṛ babūl ke, ām kahāṅ se khāë? Dohā.
s. f. The name of a tree of the Mimosa kind. (There are several species, but the commonest is Arabica. Roxb. ) The acacia tree.
subs: lit. 'To plant a babūl tree'; to do something conducive to harm or to a bad end.

def: s.m. Whirlwind (syn. bagūlā).

s.f. The gum-acacia tree, Mimosa arabica (the wood of which is commonly used in making cart-wheels, agricultural implements, tent-pegs, &c.; and the bark, being a powerful astringent, is used in tanning: syn. kīkar, mug̠īlān)
more: n. m. H. bāo wind. A. whirlwind ( bagūlā ).
subs: lit. 'To plant a babūl tree'; to do something conducive to harm or to a bad end.

def: adj. & s.m. Of or pertaining to Babylon, Babylonish; — a Babylonian.
adj. = bābulī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Of or pertaining to Babylon, Babylonish; — a Babylonian.
s.f. = baburī, q.v. prop. n. Babel, or Babylon.
s.m. Camomile; camomile flowers; — wild ivy, Anthemis nobilis.
more: n. m. The camomile; wild ivy; Anthemis nobilis . Wat.

def: s.f. The gum-acacia tree, Mimosa arabica (the wood of which is commonly used in making cart-wheels, agricultural implements, tent-pegs, &c.; and the bark, being a powerful astringent, is used in tanning: syn. kīkar, mug̠īlān)
more: s. f. The name of a tree of the Mimosa kind. (There are several species, but the commonest is Arabica. Roxb. ) The acacia tree.
subs: lit. 'To plant a babūl tree'; to do something conducive to harm or to a bad end.

def: v. past tense 3d pers. sing. Bore or carried away.
s.f. = babūl, q.v. prop. n. Babel, or Babylon.
more: s. f. The name of a tree of the Mimosa kind. (There are several species, but the commonest is Arabica. Roxb. ) The acacia tree.

def: s.m. Child, boy (a term of endearment); clay effigy of a boy; a toy.
more: n. m. diminutive of bābū , q. v. Jiske pās ḍhebuā, wohī hamārā babuā . E. Prov. Who has the pence, is sure "Your reverence." 1. A child; boy; a term of endearment. 2. A toy; a clay effigy of a boy.

def: s.f. A kind of aromatic root; orris root; root of the sweet flag, Acorus calamus odoratus.
s.m. An inferior tribe of Rājpūts in the district of Jaunpur.

ety: imper. or rt. of baćnā, q.v.
def: intj. Take care! out of the way! bać-bać (intens.) Idem.
more: Panj. vich, n. Conṭ. of bīch , q. v.
n. f. Orris-root; the root of the sweet flag; Iris germanica , or Acorus calamus . Wat.
adv. In, among, between. See بيچ.

def: s.f. Speech, &c. = vāćā, q.v. s.f. Speech, &c. (i.q. vāć); — affirmation, assertion; agreement; promise, pledge; vow; oath.
more: r. वच Speak) s. f. 1. Speech, language, word. 2. Affirmation, agreement, promise.

def: s.m. = baććā, q.v. s.m. Young (of any creature); child, infant, babe, little one; youngster, youth, lad, boy; thoughtless, simple or inexperienced person; — young plant, sapling
more: E; n. m. S. वत्स, Pr. vachchho the young of any animal. 1. The young of any creature; child; infant; babe; baby; nurseling; suckling ( dūd pītā bachchā ); bairn; little one; brat ( phūṅsṛā ); chit; urchin; bantling; bratling. 2. Youth; boy ( laṛkā ); lad; stripling; youngster; younker; whipster. Tū kāhe mor bachwā garyāvat rahī? E. Wom. Why did you abuse my child? 3. A thoughtless; inconsiderate; inexperienced person. 4. Chicken ( chūzah ); nestling ( chiṛyā kā bachchā ). 5. Sapling; seedling; young plant ( paudā ).

adj. 1. Child-like; innocent; simple. 2. Inexperienced; thoughtless ( abujh ); inconsiderate ( an-samajh ). bachche-bāzī, lauṅḍe bāzī , n. f. Sodomy. bachchoṅ kā khel, mūṅh kā nivālā, ghar kī bāt, nānī jī kā ghar . An easy matter; a trifle; a small affair. bachchoṅ kā sā , adj. Infantile; babyish; childish; girlish; puerile. bachche-kachche , n. m. Children; little ones. bachche-kashī , n. f. Child-bearing; pregnancy. bachche-kushī , P.; bāl-hattyā , H. n. f. Infanticide. adh-banā bachchā , n. A fœtus; embryo. bachche wālī mur/?/gī , n. f. lit . The hen with her chickens. The constellation of the Pleiades.
subs: s.m. Mere child's play, mere trifle, easy matter
bać-kać, s.m. Children, little ones ( = kaćće-baćće, kać-bać)
s.f. lit. 'Hen with chickens'; the Pleiades.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَچَّه (bačča), from Middle Persian wck' (waččag, “child, baby, cub”), from Proto-Iranian *wacáh (“calf”), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *watsás.

def: s.m. (pl. of baćća), Children, little ones.
adj. & adv. Fit for children; appertaining to children; child-like, puerile, childish; — in a childish manner.

adj. & s.m. = vāćak, q.v. adj. Speaking; — declaratory, explanatory, expressive (of), significant, implying, signifying; — verbal, expressed by words or sounds; — s.m. A significant sound, a word; (in Gram.) an explanatory particle; — a speaker; a reader; — a messenger.
more: part. act. 1. Speaker. 2. (in grammar) An explanatory particle.

def: (comp. of the porf. parts of baćnā and khućnā, q.q.v.)
s.m. Speech, word, expression, utterance, talk, discourse; declaration, affirmation, saying, precept, dictum, opinion; promise, assurance; agreement, compact, covenant, vow
more: n. m. S. वचन from वच् to speak, Pr. vaaṇam . 1. Speech; word ( bāt ); talk; discourse. Nikalte haiṅ ab to khushī ke bachan, Na ho gar khushī, to nahīṅ Barhaman . Mīr Hasan. A happy omen now appears upon your sky, If happiness be not your lot, no Brahman I. 2. Vow; solemn assurance; word of honor; parole ; pledge. Bachnoṅ kā bāṅdhā khaṛā hai . Prov. Saying. True to its pledge the sky stands firm. 3. Appointment; agreement ( iqrār ); promise. 4. Boon; favour. Kekaï ne do bachan māṅge. bachan-bandh honā , v. n. To give or pledge one's word. bachan-pāl , adj. True to one's word. bachan pālnā, yā nibhānā , v. n. To stand to one's bargain; to abide by a promise; to be true to one's word; to keep faith. bachan-patr , n. m. Deed; covenant; bond; agreement ( iqrār-nāmā ). bachan toṛ'nā, yā chhoṛnā , v. n. To break one's word or promise. bachan denā , v. a. To promise; engage; agree to; pledge one…

s. m. Speech, talk, discourse, word, expression; promise, agreement. بچن بندکرلينا bachan band-kar lenā or بچن بنده کرنا bachan bandh karnā, To bind by promise. بچن بنده هونا bachan bandh honā, To give oneʼs word, to promise, to be agreed upon. بچن پالنا bachan pālnā, To abide by a promise. بچن توڙنا bachan toṛnā, To break oneʼs word. بچن چهوڙنا bachan chhoṛnā, To break oneʼs promise. بچن دينا bachan denā, To promise, to agree. بچن ڐالنا bachan ḍālnā, To question, to ask, to inquire. بچن لينا bachan lenā, To receive a promise. بچن مارنا bachan mārnā, To conclude an agreement. بچن ماننا bachan mānnā, To obey. بچن نبهانا bachan nibhānā, To abide by a promise. بچن هارنا bachan hārnā, To promise, to agree, to give or pledge or plight oneʼs word, to affirm.
subs: adj. Bound by promise, &c., pledged; agreed upon, stipulated, &c.; — baćan-bandh karnā or kar-lenā, v.t. To bind (one) by promise, &c.; — baćan-bandh honā, v.n. To be bound by promise, &c. to give or pledge one's word, to promise; to be agreed upon
adj. Abiding by a promise; true to (one's) word; — baćan-pālnā, v.n. To keep (one's) word, abide by a promise, &c.
baćan ćhoṛnā, v.n. To break (one's) word or promise
adj. Betrothed; espoused; — s.m. One to whom a person is betrothed
(-ko), To pledge (one's) word, to promise, engage; to agree, consent
(-par). To ask or inquire (of), question; to request, apply or sue (for)
(-se), To obtain a promise, take a covenant (from)
v.n. To conclude an agreement; to promise
(-kā), To heed or mind the word (of), to obey; to comply, consent
(apnā), To give, pledge, or plight (one's) word; to promise; to agree to; to affirm, declare; — to break (one's) word or promise (a rare signification).

ety: S. वर्जयति rt. वृज् or वृच्, whence H. बच —
def: v.t. To save, preserve, rescue, deliver, extricate, set free; to sustain, support, protect; to secure, defend, guard, shield, screen, cloak; to conceal, hide; to set aside, put or lay by, save, spare, leave; to reserve, keep back (a part of anything fraudulently); to avoid, keep clear of, move out of the way
more: v. a. H. bīch ānā to come between. 1. To save; protect; shield; defend; ward off; beat off; preserve; keep. 1. Jisko Khudā bachāë, us par kabhī na āfat āë . Prov. On him whom God befriends no ill descends. 2. Hāth pair bachāīye, mūzū ko ṭarkāīye . Prov. Save yourself and spare the foe. 3. Āp ko bachātā hai, garīb ko phaṅsātā hai . Shields himself and snares the poor. 2. To maintain; support; sustain. 3. To screen; conceal; cover; hide. 4. To crib; embezzle; defraud; steal. Rupaë meṅ chār āne bachātā hai . 5. To assist; aid; help; to hold out a helping hand. 6. To extricate; deliver; free; liberate. 7. ( In law ). To set free; liberate; emancipate; redeem. 8. To lay by; save up; put by; hoard. Kaho, kuchh bachāte bhī ho, yā sab hī uṛā dete ho? 9. To clear the way; get out of the way. Chhakṛe-wāle, bachāīyo! Carter, look out! bachā jānā , v. a. To get off; escape; abscond. Tum har dafa gol…

v. a. 1. (trans. of بچنا) To save, to preserve, to protect, secure, to reserve, extricate, to spare, to defend, to guard, to leave, to avoid, to conceal. 2. (Perhaps, for بجانا q. v.) To sound (a musical instrument).
subs: v.n. To keep (oneself) clear of, to avoid, escape, get off scot free
(-ko), To carry off safe, to save, secure
baćāne-hārā, baćāne-hār, baćān-hār, s.m. Deliverer, saviour, preserver, protector.
wety: Cognate to Punjabi ਬਚਾਉਣਾ (bacāuṇā) / بَچاؤݨا (bacā'oṇā), Marathi वाचवणे (vāċavṇe). Urdu verb set بچنا بچانا

def: s.f. = bāteṅ (the word occurs in the Rāmāyaṇ).

s.m. Preservation, salvation, deliverance, rescue, extrication, liberation; escape; protection, defence, refuge; guarding, shielding, screening, cloaking; reserve, reservation, avoidance; contrivance, evasion, pretext; — capacity, amplitude
more: n. m. 1. Guard; defence; protection; asylum; refuge ( panāh ). 2. Security; safety; safe-conduct; safeguard. 3. Liberation (chhuṭkārā); deliverance (rihāī); extrication; rescue. 4. Escape; retreat; flight. 5. Excuse; plea; apology. 6. Evasion; pretext; pretence; make-believe. bachāo karnā, bachāo nikālnā , v. a. 1. To contrive; plan; provide; arrange for. 2. To shield; shelter; screen; protect.
(from بچنا) s. m. Protection, defence, safety, security, preservation, salvation, escape.
subs: (-se, or -meṅ), To save or guard oneself (from); to provide (against); to effect the deliverance (of, -kā), to save, protect, guard, shield, screen; to practise reserve, be reserved
(-kā, of, a person, -se, from, or -meṅ, in, a difficulty, &c.), To contrive the escape, deliverance, &c. (of), &c. = baćāʼo karnā, q.v.

def: v.t. = baćānā, q.v. v.t. To save, preserve, rescue, deliver, extricate, set free; to sustain, support, protect; to secure, defend, guard, shield, screen, cloak; to conceal, hide; to set aside, put or lay by, save, spare, leave; to reserve, keep back (a part of anything fraudulently); to avoid, keep clear of, move out of the way
subs: v.n. To keep (oneself) clear of, to avoid, escape, get off scot free
(-ko), To carry off safe, to save, secure
baćāne-hārā, baćāne-hār, baćān-hār, s.m. Deliverer, saviour, preserver, protector.

def: s.m. Deliverance, safety, escape ( = baćāwā, baćāʼo).
s.f. Residue, remainder, surplus; balance; savings; gain, profit.
more: n. f. 1. Balance; balance of an account; savings; residue; remainder; surplus ( baqīyā ). 2. Gain; profit ( ot ); interest. bacho, bach, bachyo , intj. Look out! ho there!

def: s.m. Preservation, salvation, deliverance, rescue, extrication, liberation; escape; protection, defence, refuge; guarding, shielding, screening, cloaking; reserve, reservation, avoidance; contrivance, evasion, pretext; — capacity, amplitude
subs: (-se, or -meṅ), To save or guard oneself (from); to provide (against); to effect the deliverance (of, -kā), to save, protect, guard, shield, screen; to practise reserve, be reserved
(-kā, of, a person, -se, from, or -meṅ, in, a difficulty, &c.), To contrive the escape, deliverance, &c. (of), &c. = baćāʼo karnā, q.v.

def: s.m. Saving, defending, guarding, protecting; preservation, &c. ( = baćā,o)
subs: v.t. To save, guard, defend, &c. ( = baćāʼo karnā).

def: s.m. Young (of any creature); child, infant, babe, little one; youngster, youth, lad, boy; thoughtless, simple or inexperienced person; — young plant, sapling
more: s. m. A child, the young of any animal. See بچه bachcha.
subs: s.m. Mere child's play, mere trifle, easy matter
bać-kać, s.m. Children, little ones ( = kaćće-baćće, kać-bać)
s.f. lit. 'Hen with chickens'; the Pleiades.

def: s.f. Infancy, childhood ( = baćpan).
direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
adj. Affectionate, kind, loving, tender, fond, indulgent; — s.m. Affection, fondness, tenderness.
s.f. Little girl; female child; maid, lassie; — female young animal; — tuft of hair on the lower lip, an imperial.
more: n. f. A little girl; lassie; miss; wench; damsel; maid; maiden; virgin; a daughter.
s. f. (from بچه bachcha ) A female young one.
wety: From بَچَّہ (child, boy) + ـی (-ī)

def: oblique plural of بَچَّہ (bacca, “kid; boy”)
s.m.? Name of a tribe of Rājpūts (cf. bać).
s.m. Calf = baćhṛā, q.v.
s.f. Selection, separation; proportionate rate, rate of distribution, assessment on a share; subscription, portion, share, division; — slit or corner of the mouth; — adj. Set aside, useless
subs: To raise by subscription; to levy a tax
To have a pain or inflammation at the corners of the lips
lit. 'The corners of the mouth to open'; to be pleased, to be delighted; to laugh intensely.

def: s.m. Calf ( = baćhṛā); young of any animal ( = baćća).
more: n. m. 1. A dog's paw. W. Lā, beṭā, bāchhā! Give me your paw, good dog. 2. (S. वत्स ) E. The young of a cow; a calf (bachhṛā) .
s. m. A calf, or the young of any animal.

def: s.m. Calf = baćh, baćhṛā.
intj. A term of endearment or defiance, as my boy! fellow! my lad! sirrah! Bhalā bachā! ab-ke ānā! My lad, just you come bachchah-dān , P.; dharan , H.; rahem , A. n. m. The womb; uterus.
more: s. m. An infant, a child, the young of any creature. (Plur. بچگان). بچه دان bachcha-dān, s. m. The womb. بچي والي مرغي bachche-wālī murg̠ẖī, The Pleiades.
s. m. A calf. See بچهرا.

def: s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts about Aligarh.
s. f. (dakh.) Plaything, toy.
s. m. (dakh.) A foal ( Harris ): see بچهيرا bachherā.
s.m. Colt; calf ( = baćhṛā).
more: Mag., n. m. A colt. Naī ghuṛyā koṭhī meṅ bachher . Mag. Prov. The strange mare wants to foal in the house. (Strange men, strange ways). nākand bachherā , n. m. lit. An unridden colt. (Met.) An inexperienced, thoughtless person ( nā-tajrabah-kār ).

def: s.m. Colt; calf ( = baćhṛā).
s.f. Foal, filly; female calf ( = baćhiyā).
more: E; n. f. 1. A foal; filly. 2. ( Slang ) A young lady; a daughter. Ghoṛī mar jātī hai, to bachherī par kāṭhī dharte haiṅ . A double entendre . Slang. Prov. When the mare is dead you may ride the filly. ☞ The unsuspecting license given by a Mullā or Divine to a wag who, under cover of the question put by him in the words of the proverb, obtained the religious sanction he wanted.
(see بچهيرا) s. f. A foal, filly.

def: s.f. Foal, filly; female calf ( = baćhiyā).

E. n. f. pl. bāchheṅ , S. वक्त्र mouth. The corner of the lip. bāchheṅ ānā , v. n. To have a pain in the corners of the lips. bāchheṅ khilnā, yā ṭhoṛī tak ānā , v. n. To laugh intensely; to die with laughter; to be delighted, overjoyed. Shādi kī sun-kar bāchheṅ khil gaīṅ .
more: n. f. H. bāṅṭ divide. Subscription; proportionate rate; proportion of tax; rate of distribution; assessment on a share; portion; share. Bāṭī ghāṭī kutyā mare, nāth kahe merī bāchhā paṛe . Prov. In street or lane a dead dog lies, the holy man a rate levies. bāchh ḍālnā , v. n. To levy a tax; raise by subscription. Gām bāchh ḍāl-kar, begā joṛ rupaīyā lāo rī . Rus. Song.
1. s. f. ( वांछा Desire) Selection. 2. ( वक्त The mouth) The corner of the lip. 3. adj. Useless.

def: s.f. Female calf, &c. ( = baćhiyā, q.v.). s.f. Female calf; young heifer; daughter, dear girl (a term of endearment among women); — a Hindu ceremony performed on the thirteenth or seventeenth day after a death
s.f. Female calf; young heifer; daughter, dear girl (a term of endearment among women); — a Hindu ceremony performed on the thirteenth or seventeenth day after a death
more: E.; n. f. H. bachchā . 1. A female calf; an heifer. 2. ( bachhṛī ) A daughter ( beṭī ); a girl. Hin. Wom. Arī merī bachhiyā! tū kahāṅ gaī rī! A dirge. Oh! my child! my child! where art thou gone! 3. A Hindu ceremony performed on the 13th or 17th day after one's death. bachhyā kā bāvā , n. m. An ox ( bail ); a goose; a fool; a simpleton. Bachhyā kā bāvā hī rahā . Prov. Saying. The silly ox remained an ox.

def: adj. Appertaining or relating to bachchā ; small (generally shoes).
more: n. m. bachkānī , n. f. ( Slang ). A girl adopted by an old prostitute. A young girl; a lass.

def: v.t. To choose, select, cull, separate, pick out (syn. ćunnā, ćhānṭnā, binnā).
more: Tir.; bīnnā , W., v. a. S. वाञ्छ् to desire. To choose; select; pick out the best ( chunnā ). Achchhe pān bāchh lo . E.
v. a. To choose, to select.

def: s.m. = baćnāg, q.v. s.m. The plant Aconitum ferox (found at high elevations in the Himalaya and Nepaul); the root of Aconite, and the deadly poison obtained from it (also written baćhnāg).
more: s. m. Name of a vegetable poison.

def: s.m. = baćhṛā, q.v.
more: Brij. n. m. H. bachchā the young of an animal. 1. A calf ( jaingṛā ). 2. A son ( beṭā ) (an endearment). Wom. Hāë!-hāë! mere bachhṛe, tujhe kis balā ne khā līyā . A dirge. Rusūm-i-Hind. 3. A green horn; a simpleton. ( Rare ). 4. A young or youthful person. Sīṅgh kaṭā, bachhṛoṅ meṅ milā . Prov. His horns cut off he mixes with the calves. (Said of an old man got up as a young one).

def: s.m. Calf; child, boy (a term of endearment used by women); — moon-calf, fool (syn. baććā, baćhuʼā).
s.m. = baćhṛū, baćhṛā, q.q.v.
s.m. Calf ( = baćhṛā).

s.m. Calf; young of an animal, &c. ( = baććā, q.v.). s.m. Young (of any creature); child, infant, babe, little one; youngster, youth, lad, boy; thoughtless, simple or inexperienced person; — young plant, sapling
more: n. m. 1. A kind of dagger; a small stiletto with a curved blade. 2. An ornament worn on the toes (E. bichhyā ). 3. W. The seed pod of san (hemp).
s. m. 1. A sort of dagger. 2. An ornament worn on the toes.
s. m. A ring worn round the toes, &c.: see Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. xxvii.
subs: s.m. Mere child's play, mere trifle, easy matter
bać-kać, s.m. Children, little ones ( = kaćće-baćće, kać-bać)
s.f. lit. 'Hen with chickens'; the Pleiades.

def: intj. Out of the way, &c. = baćo, bać, q.q.v. intj. Out of the way! take care! look out! ( = bać, q.v.). intj. Take care! out of the way! bać-bać (intens.) Idem.
s.m. See s.v. baććā. s.m. Young (of any creature); child, infant, babe, little one; youngster, youth, lad, boy; thoughtless, simple or inexperienced person; — young plant, sapling
subs: s.m. Mere child's play, mere trifle, easy matter
bać-kać, s.m. Children, little ones ( = kaćće-baćće, kać-bać)
s.f. lit. 'Hen with chickens'; the Pleiades.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Appertaining to, or fit for, children; small, diminutive (generally applied to shoes); — s.m. A pair of child's shoes; — a dancing boy (syn. bhagtiyā).
more: (trans. of بچکنا) v. a. To balk, to disappoint, to break oneʼs promise.
q. v.) 1. adj. Small (generally applied to shoes and clothes). 2. s. m. A dancing boy.
(caus. of بچکنا bichaknā ) v. a. (dakh.) To frighten, fear, affright, daunt ( Harris ).

def: s.f. Little girl, young girl; a girl adopted by an old prostitute.
more: (perhaps, from بچه) s. f. A girl adopted by an old prostitute.
